$comment: DRAFT agent manifest — prepared for publication to an open agent registry (e.g. Sentient GRID). Status: draft; not yet published. The agent itself runs in production inside AqueductX today (atlas/scripts/scout-ethichub.mjs); this manifest is the composability surface for running it as a standalone, registry-listed agent.
name: scout-ethichub
description: Reads public smallholder coffee-lot listings from EthicHub's shop surface and emits canonical, content-addressed lot records with dated provenance. One of AqueductX's swarm roles: the scout performs the 'aggregate' step of aggregate → verify → price → publish → fill → settle.
task_shape: Given a public marketplace/lending surface, return canonical lot records a stranger can re-verify: commodity, weight, origin (community-approximate coordinates only — plot geometry never leaves the producer's side, see privacy contract), price as published, quality score where published, and a source URL + fetch timestamp on every record.

outputs.name: lots
outputs.schema: atlas/src/aqueduct/schema/canonicalLot.mjs — schema and content-addressed lot-ID spec are CC0 public-domain dedicated (distinct from the MIT code grant): the namespace is the algorithm, not us
outputs.provenance: every record carries provenance (LIVE | SNAPSHOT) and fetched_at; names redacted to initials by design

honesty_contract: never emits a capability or field it did not actually read (no invented certs, grades, or coordinates)
renders compliance status, never plot geometry (docs/research/13-privacy-and-swarm-coordination.md)
producer names appear as initials only (redactName is a policy enforcement point, not cosmetic truncation)
runtime: node >= 20, no API keys required (public reads only)
